review_summary: Transcript is sufficient for distinguishing sequence-modeling regimes and RNN motivation. The concepts are explicitly described in words, so no clip is needed.

question_bank.question: Which task is a one-to-many sequence modeling example?
choices: An image mapped to a variable-length caption
A video mapped to one class label
A fixed vector mapped to another fixed vector
A set of frames mapped to one average embedding
question_bank.explanation: One-to-many starts from one fixed input and generates a sequence as output, such as turning an image into a caption.

question_bank.question: Which scenario is many-to-one sequence modeling?
choices: One image produces one caption token at a time
Multiple video frames produce one class label
Each frame produces one label at the same time step
A word sequence produces an equally long tag sequence
question_bank.explanation: Many-to-one uses a sequence as input and collapses it into one output, such as classifying an entire video from multiple frames.

transcript_quotes: what is this a video of

question_bank.question: Why is the aligned many-to-many setting often used first to explain RNN mechanics?
choices: It guarantees the shortest possible sequences.
It makes every hidden state independent.
Each input step has a corresponding output step.
It removes the need for hidden states.
question_bank.explanation: Aligned many-to-many is easier to reason about because the model produces one output for each input time step.
